note: Additive category on top of v1/multihop (both stay frozen). Precise/paraphrase queries in v1 skew toward the exact query type cosine embeddings are built to nail — this category tests loose, casual, imprecisely-worded queries closer to how memories are actually queried day-to-day, which is the specific case the sigma/Bhattacharyya machinery targets (README: 'a vague exploratory question casts wider'). Authored against real, previously-confirmed memory IDs before any run. v2 (2026-07-09 evening): q33 and q42 replaced after close-read audit found real defects — q33's original target likely captured an assistant aside during architecture discussion, not a decision the user ever consciously weighed (no real query would expect to recall it); q42's original query was genuinely ambiguous, since multiple distinct 'scoring fix' memories exist in the corpus and nothing in the query disambiguates which. q36/q43/q44 were also flagged during the same audit but are confirmed genuine system gaps, not gold defects — left unchanged as fair hard cases.

'spread'/'widened' are terms specific to this memory's actual text, not shared by the others.
